title: The In-Between Move
summary: Insert a stronger threat before the obvious recapture

steps.title: Don't Just React
steps.text: A zwischenzug ('in-between move', from German) is a surprising move inserted into an expected sequence — usually a check or a bigger threat — that changes the position before the obvious move is played. It's the mark of strong tactical vision.

steps.title: Example Pattern
steps.text: When your opponent captures something of yours, your reflex is to recapture. But sometimes a zwischenzug — a check elsewhere, or a bigger threat — wins more material. Always consider: before I recapture, is there something BETTER? Even a single intervening check can win the game.
